methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Panthalassa is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Garth Sheldon-Coulson. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company operating in the Climate & Energy sector, with direct peer selection focused on same-stage private businesses.

The peer-median method was applied using three Series A climate companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). This sample yields a median valuation of $150M with a range spanning $110M to $200M. Given limited methodological diversity in available data, this peer-median result serves as the primary reference point for triangulation.

The valuation analysis supports a range of $128M to $172M, centered on a median of $150M. This range reflects the $40M spread observed across the three direct comparables and represents a ±15% variance from the peer median. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of applicable peers at this stage, the absence of transaction-based or revenue multiple approaches, and potential differentiation in Panthalassa's technology or market positioning relative to the comparable set. Any material divergence from peer-stage fundamentals—whether in capital intensity, technical risk, or market timing—could shift valuation materially beyond this range.
